description: Clause baseline for the security clause linter. Each entry is a clause the engine checks a vendor contract (MSA/DPA) for. 'detect.present_if' = phrases that signal the clause exists; 'detect.red_flags' = weak/one-sided language to flag even when present; 'detect.required_value' = a specific value to enforce. crown_jewels uses the division keys: parks, studios, stream, news, sports, corp, all.
generated_for: Irvens Eristil — NBCU Security Assurance / Governance prep

clauses.name: Information Security Requirements / Security Exhibit
clauses.obligation: Vendor maintains the controls listed in a security exhibit/schedule (encryption, access control, logging, patching), tied to a named standard.
clauses.elite_position: Tie the exhibit to a named standard (ISO 27001 / SOC 2 / NIST) so 'reasonable security' is a measurable bar with evidence, not an argument.

clauses.name: Right to Audit / Assessment
clauses.obligation: Customer may assess the vendor on-site, remotely, or via accepted attestations (SOC 2 Type II, TPN Gold, PCI AOC), with a reserved direct-audit right on an incident.

red_flags: no more than once per year
vendor will provide a summary in lieu of audit

clauses.elite_position: Accept SOC 2 / TPN to move fast, but reserve a direct audit on a security event or material change. Never give that right away entirely.

clauses.name: Security Incident & Breach Notification
clauses.obligation: Vendor notifies of a breach within a defined window from discovery, with defined triggers, cooperation duties, and no public disclosure without sign-off.
clauses.elite_position: Demand notice within 24-72h of DISCOVERY (not 'confirmation'), defined triggers, and a ban on third-party disclosure without sign-off.

clauses.name: Data Return & Secure Destruction
clauses.obligation: On termination, vendor returns or certifiably destroys customer data within a fixed window, with written attestation and no surviving archival copies.
clauses.elite_position: Require certified destruction within a fixed window post-termination, with written attestation. The exact gap FBCS left open.

clauses.name: Data Retention & Purpose Limitation
clauses.obligation: Vendor uses data only for the stated service, retains it only as long as needed, and never for its own product/AI training.
clauses.elite_position: Bind use to the stated service only, ban product/AI training on your data, and require deletion on a defined schedule.

clauses.name: Subcontractor / Fourth-Party Flowdown
clauses.obligation: Vendor obtains prior written approval for sub-processors and flows all security terms down to them, remaining liable for their failures.
clauses.elite_position: Require prior written approval and full flowdown; the vendor stays liable for its subs (the Anodot fourth-party lesson).

clauses.name: Limitation of Liability - breach carve-out
clauses.obligation: Data-breach, confidentiality, and IP-infringement liability are carved out of the general liability cap or given a higher super-cap.

red_flags: aggregate liability shall not exceed fees

clauses.elite_position: Carve breach/confidentiality/IP liability out of the general cap. The most-fought clause in any security negotiation.

clauses.name: Indemnification
clauses.obligation: Vendor indemnifies for losses from claims caused by its breach or IP infringement, including regulatory fines and defense costs.
clauses.elite_position: Get explicit indemnity for breaches caused by failure to meet the security exhibit, covering defense costs and regulatory penalties.

clauses.name: Cyber Insurance
clauses.obligation: Vendor carries cyber/E&O coverage at a stated minimum, provides certificates, and gives notice of lapse.
clauses.elite_position: Set coverage minimums proportional to data sensitivity, require certificates, and notice of any lapse.

clauses.name: Compliance with Laws + Framework Flowdown
clauses.obligation: Vendor complies with applicable laws and named standards (PCI AOC for payments, TPN Gold for content), with a defined evidence cadence.
clauses.elite_position: Name the exact standard and evidence cadence (annual AOC, current TPN status). 'Comply with applicable laws' alone is unenforceable.

clauses.name: Data Processing Addendum (DPA)
clauses.obligation: A DPA governs controller/processor roles, lawful processing, data-subject rights, transfer mechanisms, and tracking-tech limits. See DPA-01..14 for components.

red_flags: privacy terms to be agreed later

clauses.elite_position: For streaming/ad-tech, embed VPPA-aware terms barring disclosure of viewing data and unconsented pixel/tag deployment.

clauses.name: Access Control & Remote Access
clauses.obligation: Least privilege, MFA, and governed remote/physical access for any vendor touching the environment or facilities.
clauses.elite_position: Require MFA, just-in-time access, no shared accounts, and session logging. The HVAC-vendor-to-POS entry point lives here.

clauses.name: Vulnerability Management & Pen Testing
clauses.obligation: Patching SLAs, regular vulnerability scans, and annual third-party penetration testing with results shared on request.
clauses.elite_position: Tie critical-vuln remediation to a fixed SLA, require annual third-party pen tests, and the right to a summary of findings.

clauses.name: Business Continuity / Availability SLA
clauses.obligation: Defined availability commitments, tested DR, financial penalties, and heightened obligations during high-availability windows.
clauses.elite_position: Set uptime SLAs with real financial penalties, require tested failover, and add heightened obligations during marquee live-event windows.

clauses.name: Purpose Limitation & Standard of Processing
clauses.obligation: Vendor processes data only for limited, specific business purposes, on documented instructions, to a defined standard of care.
clauses.elite_position: Define the permitted purpose narrowly in the contract and require processing strictly on documented instructions. Anything outside is a breach.

clauses.name: No-Sale / No Secondary Use
clauses.source: Privacy Addendum (uploaded) - No rights to process
clauses.obligation: Vendor shall not sell personal data or process it for its own purposes, including training its own products/AI.
clauses.elite_position: Explicit 'shall not sell or share' + 'no use beyond this relationship' + a ban on training the vendor's products/AI. Non-negotiable for ad-tech.

clauses.name: Confidentiality & Personnel Reliability
clauses.source: Privacy Addendum (uploaded) - Reliability of authorized personnel
clauses.obligation: Vendor maintains confidentiality, limits access to authorized personnel, and ensures their reliability via vetting and background checks.
clauses.elite_position: Confidentiality surviving termination, role-based least-privilege access, and background checks for anyone touching sensitive content or source material.

clauses.name: Subprocessor Approval & Notice
clauses.obligation: Vendor gives written notice with full processing detail and obtains written approval before appointing any subprocessor; no transfer of data to anyone but the customer.

red_flags: list of subprocessors on its website

clauses.elite_position: Keep approval prospective and specific: name, location, exact processing scope per subprocessor. A website list is not approval.

clauses.name: Consumer Rights / DSAR Assistance
clauses.obligation: Vendor notifies of consumer requests (opt-out, deletion, limit-use), forwards them, and assists fulfillment within a fixed window (10 days per addendum).
clauses.elite_position: Pin the assistance SLA to 10 days and require the vendor to forward any consumer request or complaint immediately, not sit on it.

clauses.name: DSAR Audit & Records
clauses.obligation: Vendor keeps complete, accurate records of consumer-request handling and grants audit access to records, systems, and personnel involved.
clauses.elite_position: Reserve a right to audit consumer-request handling specifically - records, systems, and personnel - so 'we honored it' is verifiable.

clauses.name: Deletion Verification
clauses.obligation: Vendor provides documentation verifying it no longer processes data of individuals who made a valid deletion request.
clauses.elite_position: Require written confirmation of deletion per request. An unverified deletion is the same regulatory exposure as no deletion.

clauses.name: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA)
clauses.source: Privacy Addendum (uploaded) - Carry out a DPIA
clauses.obligation: Vendor conducts or supports a DPIA for high-risk processing (notably biometric and minors' data).
clauses.elite_position: Require DPIA cooperation for any new high-risk processing - especially biometric or children's data - before go-live, not after a regulator asks.

clauses.name: Data Accuracy Notification
clauses.obligation: Vendor notifies without delay if data it processes becomes inaccurate or outdated.
clauses.elite_position: Make accuracy a notification trigger, not a best-effort goal - 'without delay' upon discovering inaccurate or stale data.

clauses.name: Cross-Border Transfer Mechanisms
clauses.obligation: No transfer to other jurisdictions without an approved mechanism. UK GDPR -> EU SCCs (SCCs prevail on conflict); Switzerland -> Swiss FADP.
clauses.elite_position: Default to data residency. Where transfers are needed, attach current SCCs plus UK/Swiss addenda, and state SCCs override on conflict.

clauses.name: Written Information Security Plan (WISP)
clauses.obligation: Vendor maintains a written security plan with administrative, physical, and technical safeguards, mapped to a named accepted framework.
clauses.elite_position: Require the WISP to map to a specific accepted framework and to be produced on request. A 'program' with no written plan is unauditable.

clauses.name: Compliance Demonstration & Monitoring
clauses.obligation: Vendor makes information available to prove compliance, monitors via manual reviews/automated scans/assessments at least annually, and provides audit reports on request.
clauses.elite_position: Tie monitoring to a 12-month cadence and require the audit report, not just an assertion. Reserve a right to remediate unauthorized use.

clauses.name: 24/7 Security Contact & 24-Hour Breach Notice
clauses.obligation: Named security contact available 24/7/365; breach notice within 24 hours of suspicion or awareness; coordinate, cooperate, remediate; no third-party disclosure without consent; cooperate in litigation at own expense.

present_if: shall not inform any third party

clauses.elite_position: Hold the 24-hour line, the named 24/7 contact, and the gag on third-party disclosure. This is the clause that rewrites the FBCS 5-month ending.

clauses.name: Accuracy of Representations
clauses.source: Privacy Addendum (uploaded) - Info provided must be true
clauses.obligation: All information the vendor represents about its security and processing must be true; a misrepresentation is itself a breach.

red_flags: to the best of its knowledge

clauses.elite_position: Keep an explicit truthfulness rep so a false questionnaire answer or stale attestation is a breach, not just an awkward conversation.
